candidates.stmt: i have lived in Stanmore for over 50 years.  I used to have a responsible job in the BBC, Our daughter went to NLCS. I know Stanmore like the back of my hand. The Tories have messed up on council tax and the environment. We have a real chance of winning this ward

I have lived in Stanmore for over 50 years. I worked as a criminal barrister prosecuting and defending. I have been chair of Harrow LibDems. I have also worked as a financial journalist. As a trustee for many years I know how to look after people's money. I am on the Council of one of the Mosaic Synagogues and was recently elected to the finance committee. I know how politics work having been a pundit on BBC during the expenses scandal.  Anyone who has been following politics in Harrow will know how appallingly the old parties have handled its finances. Listening on the doorstep I believe the LibDems have a real chance of winning in Canons ward.

My main points are trying to combat increasing antisocial behaviour and crime , town planning and its inefficiency, wasteful spending by councils , flytipping and general rubbish on streets creating an unhealthy environment and example for children, more long term planning rather than short term ineffective vote grabbing wasteful projects.

I worked as a Civil Servant for 15 years, delivering complex work across public services and coordinating projects. These are skills I will use to get things done locally.

I subsequently worked in local schools in Harrow, supporting young people with their futures, and I lead a London-wide writing group, bringing people together and helping them thrive.

As a local campaigner, I regularly speak to residents about issues like safety, clean streets, and the cost of living, and I am focused on turning those conversations into action. Having lived in Harrow most of my life I understand the area and am determined to deliver real change.

As a transgender woman who has experienced abuse on our streets I am determined to stand up for a borough where everyone can live safely, with dignity and respect. I am standing for the Green Party to build a fairer, greener Harrow for all.

Dear residents and businesses of Harrow Weald,

Harrow Weald is more than just a place — it’s our shared home, built on hard work, community spirit, and local pride. But I know many of you feel that everyday challenges are being overlooked.

Residents want safer streets, cleaner parks, reliable healthcare, and housing that respects the character of our area. Families want better opportunities for young people, less congestion on our roads, and neighbourhoods they can feel proud of.

Our local businesses are the backbone of Harrow Weald. They need better support — fair access, safer high streets, reduced anti-social behaviour, and a thriving local economy where they can grow and succeed.

I am standing to listen and to act.

My commitment is simple:
• Safer, cleaner streets for everyone
• Stronger support for local businesses
• Better access to healthcare and community services
• Sensible planning that protects our area while allowing it to grow responsibly

Together, we can build a Harrow Weald that works for everyone — residents, families, and businesses alike.

Vote for a stronger, local voice. Vote for change.

Ahsan Bin Talib
A Real Voice for Harrow Weald

I’m standing to serve as a local councillor because I care deeply about Harrow and the public services that hold our community together.

I’ve lived in Harrow for many years and, like so many local families, we rely on our public services every day – our children go to local schools, we swim in local pools, and we enjoy Harrow’s parks and green spaces. When these services work well, they change lives. When they don’t, it’s our young people and most vulnerable residents who feel it first.

For over 26 years, I’ve worked in local state schools as a social sciences teacher, and as Shadow Portfolio Holder for Children and Education Services I’ve brought that frontline experience directly into local politics. I’ve seen first-hand the pressures facing families, schools and young people – and I know how much difference early support, well-funded services and joined-up thinking can make.

Alongside my professional life, I’ve always been active in our community, campaigning for safer streets, better public spaces and opportunities for young people. I believe local politics should be about listening, standing up for residents, and making sure decisions are driven by fairness and evidence – not ideology or short-term fixes.

I’m proud to be part of a Labour team that believes in investing in public services, backing communities, and making Harrow a place where everyone can thrive. That’s why I want to continue serving – to be a strong, experienced voice for our community and to help deliver the future Harrow deserves.

I’ve been increasingly unhappy over many years that the UK has been declining on multiple levels. Declining more rapidly. Evidence points to the incompetence of our politicians, anxiety about knife crime and the criminal justice system, increasing dysfunction everywhere we look, living beyond our means as a nation, rapidly rising population putting our public services under terrible pressure, our food security Borders and Green Belt under extreme pressures, the decline in quality of the civil service - to name just a few.

My overarching feeling is that our politicians and most institutions of the state no longer represent us, are no longer fit for purpose, are no longer good for us, indeed are betraying us by causing our decline.

Many serious reforms are urgently required at every institutional level. QUESTION: Which institutions? ANSWER: – all of them: the Head of State and the Royal family, the House of Lords, House of Commons, Departments of State, Government agencies, devolved government, local government at all levels, decision making processes. It will take 6 years to enact reforms, but this is now absolutely urgent and vital for the better functioning of our State.

I, Pamela Fitzpatrick, will always stand up for the people of Harrow West.

My name is Pamela Fitzpatrick, and I am standing as an independent Parliamentary candidate for Harrow West, a place where I live, work and raise my family. I am the founder of a local charity that was established in 2010, dedicated to providing free legal advice, and I am proud to have helped thousands with housing, benefits, and immigration issues. I served as a local councillor, and as a Chair of Governors, and I am a trustee for a youth charity, all roles through which I have directly witnessed the challenges we face from service cuts, extended GP wait times, and the lack of adequate support for children with special needs. I am deeply concerned about Harrow's housing crisis, which is exacerbated by the development of luxury properties that remain out of reach for most and affect many, but especially those in the private rented sector. As a member of our diverse community, I am acutely aware of the far-reaching effects of global conflicts, which underscores the importance of compassionate and understanding leadership. As a member of the Palestine Solidarity Campaign, and a director of the Peace and Justice Project, I am a committed advocate for social and economic justice, peace, and human rights in Harrow and beyond.

Sheila has lived in Marlborough ward for 20 years. Working in the charity and housing sectors, she is a committed community campaigner, was on the committee of the No To Tesco Towers housing campaign and is the Chair of her residents' association. She organised an anti far-right rally in North Harrow, and leads a local group for Palestinian human rights.

Harrow resident for over 30 years, Asha has 19 years of experience in the charity sector, community work and activism. She has dedicated her life to creating safer spaces for the community in Harrow. A single mother, she understands the pressures residents face and how urgently local services need to improve.

Roxeth is a community built on hard work, strong families, and shared values. I am standing because I believe our area really deserves focused, practical leadership that listens, acts, and delivers real results.

Every street, every resident, every concern matters and I will promise persistence. You won’t have to wonder what I’m doing—I’ll make sure you know.

I am committed to keeping our streets safe, supporting local businesses, and ensuring our neighbourhood remains clean, well-maintained, and a place we are all proud to call home. I believe in responsible spending, protecting local services, and making sure every decision reflects the priorities of residents.

What sets me apart is simple: I will be visible, approachable, and accountable. I am not here just to represent you—I am here to work with you. Your concerns will be my priorities. I will be a councillor you see, not just a name on a leaflet. I will respond, follow up, and push for action until things get done.

Together, we can strengthen Roxeth, protect what matters most, and build a future we can all be confident in.

Let’s build a stronger sense of pride in where we live. I would be honoured to have your support.

Thank you.

Raised and educated in Harrow, Kirsten’s activism is focused on tackling Harrow’s housing crisis and empowering young people to influence their community.

Kirsten is committed to ensuring residents understand how local government works, feel included in decisions, and have a transparent, accessible councillor.

A Harrow resident of 27 years, Farid has spent 20 years supporting vulnerable people through local and regional charities. Today, he leads a charity helping local children with maths, English, coding and wellbeing.

He will bring this lifelong commitment to practical, compassionate action to the council, fighting for every resident.

Yihya works in education. Living in Harrow for over 20 years, he has long-standing community ties and involvement in community initiatives, including on food poverty, luxury housing developments that ignore the needs of local people, and supporting vulnerable residents.

He is a strong supporter of local businesses, promoting their role in building a thriving and resilient community.

Hello, my name is Caidian Johnson. 
I’m a community-focused leader with a background in psychology, conflict resolution, and youth support. Over the years I’ve worked directly with young people and families, helping them navigate stress, identity, and difficult environments.

Practical Leadership - Leadership is action, not slogans. I believe in building systems, delivering outcomes, and creating solutions that people can see and feel in their daily lives.

Local Action - My focus is Stanmore and only Stanmore: I am focused on making it a more thriving place to live by reducing traffic and improving disabled access to Stanmore train station.

Thank you — I look forward to serving and building with you

Born and raised in Harrow, Faran's work has included helping people into emergency housing, and he regularly volunteers at a Wealdstone-based charity assisting families in poverty.

Faran is dedicated to supporting local businesses and wants to work on revitalising Wealdstone High Street.

Wealdstone native Dante has experience supporting residents by working at food banks and advocating for private renters against rogue landlords.

He has campaigned in Harrow against racism and food poverty over recent years and is secretary of a local organisation for Palestinian rights.

A Wealdstone South resident for 40 years, Paul has seen the years of neglect in the area. As a bricklayer, he is frustrated at the crumbling infrastructure and feels passionately that it is time to fight for Wealdstone.

He was chair of Harrow Trades Council, having advocated for workers throughout his life, and now regularly volunteers at a charity helping families in Wealdstone.

Wealdstone South resident Aghileh founded a local organisation providing essential support to over 100 families in need every week, which has twice been shortlisted for a Harrow Heroes award.

She has been a school governor and is a trustee of a local pre-school.

Ian Price, 59, is a Business Psychologist and has lived in Pinner for 25 years. He is married to Iyabo with one adult son, Alex. Active in the local community, he has been on the Committee of Hatch End Lawn Tennis Club since 2015.
